generatedSentence: Captain Whacker, a janitor in wooly pyjama, weakly masked his offhand dread, watching the universe decay. News dripped from the helipad; only a hologram showed an unhatched shark, its facade cracking from abject generational neglect. He wished to uncloak its secrets, tennis tickets for a hackney winter. A raccoon and whitey lioness, always together, ditched their forever furniture, fleeing mansions posted twice for warlocks. The captain, moreover, felt constantly hurting, his opinion of the opponent, a hunter in a puffy pizza-stained jacket, quickly champed. He knew easily a pitfall ensured their evermore obligation: destroy the powerplant midweek. The guild gunfight struck directly, variations of aqua jewfish instead of bullets. They adjudge wonder as the outcast warlocks rewind time, their obligation a pot of nasturtium. However, a huge mick gorilla, swinging a bandsaw, danced wildly across the kilometer-wide terrarium, pocketing gaming tickets from villager archives. Through the fireplace, newest eagles flew, knitting kinship with the violin's guarantee. The suspicious origin story, long unclose, was revealed: physics dictated their intergalactic affability. In riverside restaurants, beers clinked, ensuring no decay, only a sauceboat of truth, forever binding their universe.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a grizzled space captain wearing a patched wooly pyjama and a silver chrome helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The captain, weakly observing a holographic unhatched shark projected on a decaying helipad, from a low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A raccoon and a whitey lioness, looking distressed, racing past abandoned futuristic furniture and towering mansions, captured in a dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A chaotic gunfight with aqua projectiles, focusing on the captain defending a futuristic powerplant, with a large gorilla-like figure in the background wielding a bandsaw, depicted from a wide-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The captain, now looking content, sharing beers and a sauceboat with the raccoon, lioness, and an eagle, in a riverside restaurant, rendered from a warm, inviting eye-level shot.

definitions.definition: Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
Frail, sickly or of a delicate constitution; weak.
definitions.example: 1885, I lay in weakly case and confined to my bed for four months before I was able to rise and health returned to me. — Sir Richard Burton, The Book of the Thousand Nights and a Night, Night 18

definitions.definition: One who, or something which, whacks.
Anything large; a whopper.
A sweater or similar garment made of wool.
A sheep not yet shorn.
A piece of woolwork.
Made of wool.
definitions.example: Put on a woolly jumper and turn down the thermostat.

definitions.definition: Having a thick, soft texture, as if made of wool.
definitions.example: There was nothing left in the fruit bowl but a brown banana and a couple of woolly pears.

definitions.definition: (of thinking, principles, etc.) Based on emotions rather than logic.
definitions.example: That's the sort of woolly thinking that causes wars to start.

definitions.definition: Unclear, fuzzy, hazy, cloudy.
Clothed in wool.
A woolly back; someone from the area around Liverpool, not from Liverpool itself.
Clothes for wearing to bed and sleeping in, usually consisting of a loose-fitting shirt and pants/trousers.
Loose-fitting trousers worn by both sexes in various southern Asian countries including India.
Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

definitions.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

definitions.definition: An entity similar to our universe; one component of a larger entity known as the multiverse.
Everything under consideration.
definitions.example: In all this universe of possibilities, there is only one feasible option.

definitions.definition: A sample taken from the population.
An imaginary collection of worlds.
definitions.example: The universe in this comic book series is richly imagined.

definitions.definition: (films) A collection of stories with characters and settings that are less interrelated than those of sequels or prequels.
A whole world, in the sense of perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: That didn’t just rock my world, it rocked my universe.

definitions.definition: Not yet hatched.
definitions.example: an unhatched egg

definitions.definition: Not shaded with hatching.
An ordinary horse.
A carriage for hire or a cab.
A horse used to ride or drive.
A breed of English horse.
A hired drudge; a hireling; a prostitute.
To make uninteresting or trite by frequent use.
To use as a hackney.
To carry in a hackney coach.
Offered for hire; hence, much used; trite; mean.
definitions.example: hackney authors

definitions.definition: A small landing place for helicopters, denoted by a large "H".
A three-dimensional image of an object created by holography.
Without planning or thinking ahead.
definitions.example: She gave an offhand speech.

definitions.definition: Careless; without sufficient thought or consideration.
definitions.example: He doesn't realise how hurtful his offhand remarks can be.

definitions.definition: Curt, abrupt, unfriendly.
definitions.example: She was quite offhand with me yesterday.

definitions.definition: Right away, immediately, without thinking about it.
definitions.example: Offhand, I'd guess that that's a yellow-bellied sapsucker.

definitions.definition: In an offhand manner.
An only child.
Alone in a category.
definitions.example: He is the only doctor for miles.

definitions.definition: Singularly superior; the best.
Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.
definitions.example: He is their only son, in fact, an only child.

definitions.definition: Mere.
Without others or anything further; exclusively.
definitions.example: My heart is hers, and hers only.

definitions.definition: No more than; just.
definitions.example: If there were only one more ticket!

definitions.definition: As recently as.
definitions.example: He left only moments ago.

definitions.definition: Used to express surprise or consternation at an action.
definitions.example: She's only gone and run off with the milkman!

definitions.definition: Introduces a disappointing or surprising outcome that renders futile something previously mentioned. See also only to, only for.
definitions.example: I helped him out only for him to betray me.

definitions.definition: Above all others; particularly.
Under the condition that; but.
definitions.example: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

definitions.definition: But; except.
definitions.example: I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.

definitions.definition: The face of a building, especially the front view or elevation.
(by extension) The face or front (most visible side) of any other thing, such as an organ.
A deceptive or insincere outward appearance; a front.
An object serving as a simplified interface to a larger body of code, as in the facade pattern.
To fall one drop at a time.
definitions.example: Listening to the tap next door drip all night drove me mad!

definitions.definition: To leak slowly.
definitions.example: Does the sink drip, or have I just spilt water over the floor?

definitions.definition: To let fall in drops.
definitions.example: After putting oil on the side of the salad, the chef should drip a little vinegar in the oil.

definitions.definition: (usually with with) To have a superabundance of valuable things.
definitions.example: The Old Hall simply drips with masterpieces of the Flemish painters.

definitions.definition: (of the weather) To rain lightly.
definitions.example: The weather isn't so bad. I mean, it's dripping, but you're not going to get so wet.

definitions.definition: To be wet, to be soaked.
To whine or complain consistently; to grumble.
The process or result of being gradually decomposed.
A deterioration of condition; loss of status or fortune.
To deteriorate, to get worse, to lose strength or health, to decline in quality.
definitions.example: The pair loved to take pictures in the decaying hospital on forty-third street.

definitions.definition: (of organic material) To rot, to go bad.
definitions.example: The cat's body decayed rapidly.

definitions.definition: (of an unstable atom) To change by undergoing fission, by emitting radiation, or by capturing or losing one or more electrons.
(of a quantum system) To undergo optical decay, that is, to relax to a less excited state, usually by emitting a photon or phonon.
Loss of airspeed due to drag.
To cause to rot or deteriorate.
definitions.example: The extreme humidity decayed the wooden sculptures in the museum's collection in a matter of years.

definitions.definition: A disc jockey.
To perform as a disc jockey.
Of, pertaining to, or changing over generations.
New information of interest.
definitions.example: Is there any news about the storm?

definitions.definition: Information about current events disseminated via media.
definitions.example: Did you hear/read/see the latest news?

definitions.definition: Posts published on newsgroups
To report; to make known.
Someone who looks after the maintenance and cleaning of a public building.
A doorman.
A person in the lowest and most despicable condition; a castaway; outcast.
Sunk to or existing in a low condition, state, or position.
Cast down in spirit or hope; degraded; servile; grovelling; despicable; lacking courage; offered in a humble and often ingratiating spirit.
Showing utter hopelessness, helplessness; showing resignation; wretched.
Rejected; cast aside.
A scaleless, predatory fish of the superorder Selachimorpha, with a cartilaginous skeleton and 5 to 7 gill slits on each side of its head.
To fish for sharks.
To remove a cloak or cover from; to deprive of a cloak or cover; to unmask; to reveal.
To remove one's cloak.
To become visible again by turning off a cloaking device.
A sport played by two players (or four in doubles), who alternately strike the ball over a net using racquets.
A match in this sport.
An earlier game in which a ball is driven to and fro, or kept in motion by striking it with a racquet or with the open hand.
To play tennis.
To drive backward and forward like a tennis ball.
A pass entitling the holder to admission to a show, concert, etc.
A pass entitling the holder to board a train, a bus, a plane, or other means of transportation
A citation for a traffic violation.
A permit to operate a machine on a construction site.
A service request, used to track complaints or requests that an issue be handled. (Generally technical support related).
A list of candidates for an election, or a particular theme to a candidate's manifesto.
definitions.example: Joe has joined the party's ticket for the county elections.

definitions.definition: A solution to a problem; something that is needed.
definitions.example: I saw my first bike as my ticket to freedom.

definitions.definition: A little note or notice.
A tradesman's bill or account (hence the phrase on ticket and eventually on tick).
A label affixed to goods to show their price or description.
A certificate or token of a share in a lottery or other scheme for distributing money, goods, etc.
A visiting card.
To issue someone a ticket, as for travel or for a violation of a local or traffic law.
To mark with a ticket.
definitions.example: to ticket goods in a retail store

definitions.definition: A chief or leader.
The person lawfully in command of a ship or other vessel.
definitions.example: The captain is the last man to leave a sinking ship.

definitions.definition: An army officer with a rank between the most senior grade of lieutenant and major.
A naval officer with a rank between commander and commodore.

A commissioned officer in the United States Navy, Coast Guard, NOAA Corps, or PHS Corps of a grade superior to a commander and junior to a rear admiral (lower half). A captain is equal in grade or rank to an Army, Marine Corps, or Air Force colonel.
One of the athletes on a sports team who is designated to make decisions, and is allowed to speak for his team with a referee or official.
The leader of a group of workers.
definitions.example: John Henry said to the captain, "A man ain't nothing but a man."

definitions.definition: The head boy of a school.
A maître d', a headwaiter.
An honorific title given to a prominent person. See colonel.
To act as captain
To exercise command of a ship, aircraft or sports team.
A nocturnal omnivore native to North America, typically with a mixture of gray, brown, and black fur, a mask-like marking around the eyes and a striped tail; Procyon lotor.
Any mammal of the genus Procyon.
Any mammal of the subfamily Procyoninae, a procyonine.
Any mammal of the family Procyonidae, a procyonid.
A substantial, often approximately cuboid, piece of any substance.
definitions.example: a block of ice

definitions.definition: A chopping block; cuboid base for cutting or beheading.
definitions.example: Anne Boleyn placed her head on the block and awaited her execution.

definitions.definition: A group of urban lots of property, several acres in extent, not crossed by public streets.
definitions.example: I'm going for a walk around the block.

definitions.definition: A residential building consisting of flats.
definitions.example: a block of flats

definitions.definition: The distance from one street to another in a city that is built (approximately) to a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The place you are looking for is two long blocks east and one short block north.

definitions.definition: Interference or obstruction of cognitive processes.
The human head.
definitions.example: I'll knock your block off!

definitions.definition: A wig block: a simplified head model upon which wigs are worn.
A mould on which hats, bonnets, etc., are shaped.
A set of sheets (of paper) joined together at one end.
definitions.example: a block of 100 tickets

definitions.definition: A logical data storage unit containing one or more physical sectors (see cluster).
A region of code in a program that acts as a single unit, such as a function or loop.
A fixed-length group of bits making up part of a message.
A case with one or more sheaves/pulleys, used with ropes to increase or redirect force, for example, as part of the rigging of a sailing ship.
A portion of a macromolecule, comprising many units, that has at least one feature not present in adjacent portions.
Something that prevents something from passing.
definitions.example: There's a block in the pipe that means the water can't get through.

definitions.definition: An action to interfere with the movement of an opposing player or of the object of play (ball, puck).
A shot played by holding the bat vertically in the path of the ball, so that it loses momentum and drops to the ground.
A defensive play by one or more players meant to deflect a spiked ball back to the hitter’s court.
A joined group of four (or in some cases nine) postage stamps, forming a roughly square shape.
A section of split logs used as fuel.
Solitary confinement.
A cellblock.
The perch on which a bird of prey is kept.
A piece of hard wood on which a stereotype or electrotype plate is mounted.
A blockhead; a stupid fellow; a dolt.
A section of a railroad where the block system is used.
The position of a player or bat when guarding the wicket.
A blockhole.
The popping crease.
A discrete group of vines in a vineyard, often distinguished from others by variety, clone, canopy training method, irrigation infrastructure, or some combination thereof.
To fill (something) so that it is not possible to pass.
definitions.example: The pipe is blocked.

definitions.definition: To prevent (something or someone) from passing.
definitions.example: You're blocking the road – I can't get through!

definitions.definition: To prevent (something from happening or someone from doing something).
definitions.example: His plan to take over the business was blocked by the boss.

definitions.definition: To impede an opponent.
definitions.example: He blocked the basketball player's shot.

definitions.definition: To specify the positions and movements of the actors.
definitions.example: It was very difficult to block this scene convincingly.

definitions.definition: To hit with a block.
To play a block shot.
To disable communication via telephone, instant messaging, etc., with an undesirable someone.
definitions.example: I tried to send you a message, but you've blocked me!

definitions.definition: To wait.
definitions.example: When the condition expression is false, the thread blocks on the condition variable.

definitions.definition: To stretch or mould (a knitted item, a hat, etc.) into the desired shape.
definitions.example: I blocked the mittens by wetting them and pinning them to a shaped piece of cardboard.

definitions.definition: To shape or sketch out roughly.
An extremely long time.
definitions.example: I haven't seen him in forever!

definitions.definition: A mythical time in the infinite future that will never come.
definitions.example: Sure, I'd be happy to meet with you on the 12th of forever.

definitions.definition: Permanent, lasting
(duration) For all time, for all eternity; for a lifetime; for an infinite amount of time.
definitions.example: I shall love you forever.

definitions.definition: (duration) For a very long time, a seeming eternity.
definitions.example: We had to wait forever to get inside.

definitions.definition: (frequency) Constantly or frequently.
definitions.example: You are forever nagging me.

definitions.definition: Large movable item(s), usually in a room, which enhance(s) the room's characteristics, functionally or decoratively.
definitions.example: A chair is furniture. Sofas are also furniture.

definitions.definition: The harness, trappings etc. of a horse, hawk, or other animal.
Fittings, such as handles, of a door, coffin, or other wooden item.
The stock and forearm of a weapon.
The pieces of wood or metal put round pages of type to make proper margins and fill the spaces between the pages and the chase.
Any material on the page other than the text and pictures of stories.
To smear, daub, plaster, or impregnate, especially with dirt which becomes hard and ingrained.
To discard or abandon.
definitions.example: Once the sun came out we ditched our rain-gear and started a campfire.

definitions.definition: To deliberately crash-land an airplane on water.
definitions.example: When the second engine failed, the pilot was forced to ditch; their last location was just south of the Azores.

definitions.definition: To deliberately not attend classes; to play hookey.
definitions.example: The truant officer caught Louise ditching with her friends, and her parents were forced to pay a fine.

definitions.definition: To dig ditches.
definitions.example: Enclosure led to fuller winter employment in hedging and ditching.

definitions.definition: To dig ditches around.
definitions.example: The soldiers ditched the tent to prevent flooding.

definitions.definition: To throw into a ditch.
definitions.example: The engine was ditched and turned on its side.

definitions.definition: The act of varying; a partial change in the form, position, state, or qualities of a thing.
A related but distinct thing.
definitions.example: All of his soups are variations on a single recipe.

definitions.definition: The angular difference at the vessel between the direction of true north and magnetic north.
A line of play that differs from the original.
A technique where material is repeated with alterations to the melody, harmony, rhythm, timbre, texture, counterpoint or orchestration; but with some invariant characteristic, e.g. a ground bass.
The modification of a hereditary trait.
Deviation from the mean orbit of a heavenly body.
To make a pledge to (someone); to promise, guarantee (someone of something); to assure.
To make sure or certain of something (usually some future event or condition).
definitions.example: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.

definitions.definition: Always; forever; eternally.
At any time in the future.
A white person, a person of European descent.
A state or bout of sickness, especially induced by cannabis use.
Close to white in colour.
A female lion (animal).
A female lion (famous person regarded with interest and curiosity).
(Oxford University slang) A female visitor to a student at Oxford, especially during commemoration week.
At all times; throughout all time; since the beginning.
definitions.example: Airplanes did not always exist as a form of transportation.

definitions.definition: Constantly during a certain period, or regularly at stated intervals (opposed to sometimes or occasionally).
definitions.example: In this street, the shops always close during lunchtime.

definitions.definition: In any event.
definitions.example: I thought I could always go back to work.

definitions.definition: (sometimes with out or through) To delete or cross out; to scratch or eliminate.
definitions.example: Please strike the last sentence.

definitions.definition: (physical) To have a sharp or sudden effect.
To thrust in; to cause to enter or penetrate.
definitions.example: A tree strikes its roots deep.

definitions.definition: (personal, social) To have a sharp or severe effect.
To touch; to act by appulse.
To take down, especially in the following contexts.
To set off on a walk or trip.
definitions.example: They struck off along the river.

definitions.definition: To pass with a quick or strong effect; to dart; to penetrate.
To break forth; to commence suddenly; with into.
definitions.example: to strike into reputation;  to strike into a run

definitions.definition: To become attached to something; said of the spat of oysters.
To make and ratify.
definitions.example: to strike a bargain

definitions.definition: To level (a measure of grain, salt, etc.) with a straight instrument, scraping off what is above the level of the top.
To cut off (a mortar joint, etc.) even with the face of the wall, or inward at a slight angle.
To hit upon, or light upon, suddenly.
definitions.example: My eye struck a strange word in the text.  They soon struck the trail.

definitions.definition: (sugar-making) To lade thickened sugar cane juice from a teache into a cooler.
To stroke or pass lightly; to wave.
To advance; to cause to go forward; used only in the past participle.
To balance (a ledger or account).
Swollen or inflated in shape, as if filled with air; pillow-like.
Coming or exhaling in puffs.
definitions.example: A puffy fat man in a tracksuit came jogging around the corner.

definitions.definition: Speaking or writing in an exaggeratedly eloquent and self-important manner.
A baked Italian dish of a thinly rolled bread dough crust typically topped before baking with tomato sauce, cheese and other ingredients such as meat, vegetables or fruit
definitions.example: Want to go out for pizza tonight?

definitions.definition: A single instance of this dish
definitions.example: He ate a whole pizza!

definitions.definition: To hang (a notice) in a conspicuous manner for general review.
definitions.example: Post no bills.

definitions.definition: To hold up to public blame or reproach; to advertise opprobriously; to denounce by public proclamation.
definitions.example: to post someone for cowardice

definitions.definition: To carry (an account) from the journal to the ledger.
To inform; to give the news to; to make acquainted with the details of a subject; often with up.
To pay (a blind).
definitions.example: Since Jim was new to the game, he had to post $4 in order to receive a hand.

definitions.definition: To put content online, usually through a publicly accessible mean, such as a video channel, gallery, message board, blog etc.
To travel with relays of horses; to travel by post horses, originally as a courier.
To travel quickly; to hurry.
To send (an item of mail etc.) through the postal service.
definitions.example: Mail items posted before 7.00pm within the Central Business District and before 5.00pm outside the Central Business District will be delivered the next working day.

definitions.definition: (horse-riding) To rise and sink in the saddle, in accordance with the motion of the horse, especially in trotting.
To publish (a message) to a newsgroup, forum, blog, etc.
definitions.example: I couldn't figure it out, so I posted a question on the mailing list.

definitions.definition: To enter (a name) on a list, as for service, promotion, etc.
To assign to a station; to set; to place.
definitions.example: Post a sentinel in front of the door.

definitions.definition: A large house or building, usually built for the wealthy.
A luxurious flat (apartment).
A house provided for a clergyman; a manse.
A stopping-place during a journey; a stage.
An astrological house; a station of the moon.
One of twenty-eight sections of the sky.
(chiefly in the plural) An individual habitation or apartment within a large house or group of buildings. (Now chiefly in allusion to John 14:2.)
Any of the branches of the Rastafari movement.
An educational institution for young children, usually between ages 4 and 6; nursery school.
The elementary school grade before first grade.
The two levels between nursery and prep; the second and third years of preschool.
Two times.
(usually with "as", of a specified quality) Doubled in quantity, intensity, or degree.
A Tex-Mex dish of strips of spicy marinated meat and/or vegetables in a soft flour tortilla, often served with salad or a savoury filling.
Comfortably, without discomfort or anxiety.
Without difficulty.
definitions.example: Individuals without a family network are easily controlled.

definitions.definition: Absolutely, without question.
definitions.example: This is easily the best meal I have eaten.

definitions.definition: A male magic-user; a male witch.
To declare to be.
To deem or determine to be.
To award judicially; to assign.
In addition to what has been said; furthermore; additionally.
The middle of the week.
That happens in the middle of the week
In the middle of the week.
To be painful.
definitions.example: Does your leg still hurt? / It is starting to feel better.

definitions.definition: To cause (a creature) physical pain and/or injury.
definitions.example: If anybody hurts my little brother, I will get upset.

definitions.definition: To cause (somebody) emotional pain.
To undermine, impede, or damage.
definitions.example: This latest gaffe hurts the legislator’s reelection prospects still further.

definitions.definition: A sensation that hurts.
One who hunts game for sport or for food; a huntsman or huntswoman.
A dog used in hunting.
A horse used in hunting, especially a thoroughbred, bred and trained for hunting.
One who hunts or seeks after anything.
definitions.example: The hunter becomes the hunted.

definitions.definition: A person who bottles up their aggression and eventually releases it explosively.
A kind of spider, the huntsman or hunting spider.
A pocket watch with a spring-hinged circular metal cover that closes over the dial and crystal, protecting them from dust and scratches.
A subjective belief, judgment or perspective that a person has formed about a topic, issue, person or thing.
definitions.example: Every man is a fool in some man's opinion.

definitions.definition: The judgment or sentiment which the mind forms of persons or things; estimation.
Favorable estimation; hence, consideration; reputation; fame; public sentiment or esteem.
Obstinacy in holding to one's belief or impression; opiniativeness; conceitedness.
The formal decision, or expression of views, of a judge, an umpire, a doctor, or other party officially called upon to consider and decide upon a matter or point submitted.
(European Union law) a judicial opinion delivered by an Advocate General to the European Court of Justice where he or she proposes a legal solution to the cases for which the court is responsible
To have or express as an opinion.
One who opposes another; one who works or takes a position against someone or something; one who attempts to stop the progress of someone or something.
definitions.example: During the crackdown, many opponents of the regime were arrested.

definitions.definition: Opposing; adverse; antagonistic.
Situated in front; opposite.
To bite or chew, especially noisily or impatiently.
To damage beyond use or repair.
definitions.example: Hooligans destroy unprovoked.

definitions.definition: To neutralize, undo a property or condition.
definitions.example: Smoking destroys the natural subtlety of the palate.

definitions.definition: To put down or euthanize.
definitions.example: Destroying a rabid dog is required by law.

definitions.definition: To severely disrupt the well-being of (a person); ruin.
definitions.example: Her divorce destroyed her; she had a nervous breakdown and was severely depressed for more than a year.

definitions.definition: To defeat soundly.
To remove data.
definitions.example: The memory leak happened because we forgot to destroy the temporary lists.

definitions.definition: To sing a song poorly.
(antiphrasis) To exhaust duly and thus recreate or build up.
definitions.example: This exercise is going to destroy all parts of your shoulders.

definitions.definition: To penetrate sexually in an aggressive way.
definitions.example: She got destroyed by her lover on the carpet.

definitions.definition: In a direct manner; in a straight line or course.
definitions.example: He drove directly to the office, and didn't stop off at the petrol station.

definitions.definition: In a straightforward way; without anything intervening; not by secondary, but by direct means.
definitions.example: I'm sick of asking you to fire him, I'll just do it directly.

definitions.definition: Plainly, without circumlocution or ambiguity; absolutely; in express terms.
definitions.example: I'm going to tell Natalie directly that I love her.

definitions.definition: Exactly; just.
definitions.example: It's directly across the street.

definitions.definition: Straightforwardly; honestly.
definitions.example: He told me directly that he'd cheated on me, and how sorry he was for it.

definitions.definition: Immediately.
Soon; next; when it becomes convenient.
definitions.example: We'll go to the store directly, but first I need to finish sweeping.

definitions.definition: As soon as; immediately.
A group or association mainly of tradespeople made up of merchants, craftspeople, or artisans for mutual aid, particularly in the Middle Ages.
A corporation.
A group of diverse species that share common characteristics or habits.
An organized group of players who regularly play together in a multiplayer game.
A battle (or a duel) using small arms.
To engage in battle using small arms.
Something that causes amazement or awe; a marvel.
definitions.example: Wonders of the World seem to come in sevens.

definitions.definition: Something astonishing and seemingly inexplicable.
definitions.example: The idea was so crazy that it is a wonder that anyone went along with it.

definitions.definition: Someone very talented at something, a genius.
definitions.example: He's a wonder at cooking.

definitions.definition: The sense or emotion which can be inspired by something curious or unknown; surprise; astonishment, often with awe or reverence.
A mental pondering, a thought.
A kind of donut; a cruller.
To be affected with surprise or admiration; to be struck with astonishment; to be amazed; to marvel; often followed by at.
To ponder; to feel doubt and curiosity; to query in the mind.
definitions.example: He wondered whether penguins could fly. She had wondered this herself sometimes.

definitions.definition: Any of several fish species, principally groupers and similar-appearing fish
The compound water.
A shade of colour, usually a mix of blue and green similar to the colour turquoise.
Of a greenish-blue colour.
In the place of something (usually mentioned earlier); as a substitute or alternative.
definitions.example: I was going to go shopping, but I went dancing instead.

definitions.definition: A potential problem, hazard, or danger that is easily encountered but not immediately obvious.
definitions.example: It's usually a simple task, but you should know the pitfalls before you attempt it yourself.

definitions.definition: A type of trap consisting of a concealed hole in the ground: victims fall into the hole and are unable to escape.
An antipattern.
A flat-bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food.
Various similar open-topped vessels, particularly
Pothole, sinkhole, vertical cave e.g. Rowten Pot
Ruin or deterioration.
definitions.example: After his arrest, his prospects went to pot.

definitions.definition: An iron hat with a broad brim worn as a helmet.
A pot-shaped non-conducting (usually ceramic) stand that supports an electrified rail while insulating it from the ground.
The money available to be won in a hand of poker or a round of other games of chance; any sum of money being used as an enticement.
A favorite: a heavily-backed horse.
A plaster cast.
To put (something) into a pot.

definitions.definition: To preserve by bottling or canning.

definitions.definition: To cause a ball to fall into a pocket.
To be capable of being potted.

definitions.definition: To shoot with a firearm.
To take a pot shot, or haphazard shot, with a firearm.
To secure; gain; win; bag.
To send someone to gaol, expeditiously.
To tipple; to drink.
To drain (e.g. sugar of the molasses) in a perforated cask.
To seat a person, usually a young child, on a potty or toilet, typically during toilet teaching.
To apply a plaster cast to a broken limb.
Marijuana

A simple electromechanical device used to control resistance or voltage (often to adjust sound volume) in an electronic device by rotating or sliding when manipulated by a human thumb, screwdriver, etc.
A large, swollen, or protruding abdomen; a paunch.
A potbelly stove.
A Vietnamese Pot-bellied pig.
A small portion or dose of a liquid which is medicinal, poisonous, or magical.

definitions.definition: A shot taken at an easy or random target.
Criticism of an easy target; a cheap shot.
An old size of paper, 12.5 × 15 inches.

definitions.definition: A station built for the production of electric power; a power station.
The portion of a vehicle devoted to providing energy for motion; the engine or motor.
The act of binding oneself by a social, legal, or moral tie to someone.
A social, legal, or moral requirement, duty, contract, or promise that compels someone to follow or avoid a particular course of action.
A course of action imposed by society, law, or conscience by which someone is bound or restricted.
A legal agreement stipulating a specified action or forbearance by a party to the agreement; the document containing such agreement.

definitions.definition: With steadfastness; with resolve; in loyalty, faithfully.
In a constant manner; occurring continuously; persistently.
(frequency) Recurring regularly.
definitions.example: I find that I am constantly reminding you to feed your pets.

definitions.definition: In an unchangeable or invariable manner; in every case.
The act of rewinding.
A button or other mechanism for rewinding.
definitions.example: I meant to pause the picture, but hit the rewind by mistake.

definitions.definition: To wind (something) again.
To wind (something) back, now especially of cassette or video tape, CD, DVD etc.; to go back on a video or audio recording.
To go back or think back to a previous moment or place, or a previous point in a discourse.
An open hearth for holding a fire at the base of a chimney.
The popular name of any of the plants in the Tropaeolum genus of flowering plants native to south and central America.
A plant in this genus, Tropaeolum majus.
Any of the plants in the genus, Nasturtium, that includes watercress.
In a wide manner; across a wide area.
Commonly; generally; to a great degree.
A carnivorous spotted large cat native to South and Central America, Panthera onca.
A bag stitched to an item of clothing, used for carrying small items.
Such a receptacle seen as housing someone's money; hence, financial resources.
definitions.example: I paid for it out of my own pocket.

definitions.definition: An indention and cavity with a net sack or similar structure (into which the balls are to be struck) at each corner and one centered on each side of a pool or snooker table.
An enclosed volume of one substance surrounded by another.
definitions.example: The drilling expedition discovered a pocket of natural gas.

definitions.definition: An area of land surrounded by a loop of a river.

The area of the field to the side of the goal posts (four pockets in total on the field, one to each side of the goals at each end of the ground). The pocket is only a roughly defined area, extending from the behind post, at an angle, to perhaps about 30 meters out.
The region directly behind the offensive line in which the quarterback executes plays.
An area where military units are completely surrounded by enemy units.
The position held by a second defensive middle, where an advanced middle must retreat after making a touch on the attacking middle.
A large bag or sack formerly used for packing various articles, such as ginger, hops, or cowries; the pocket of wool held about 168 pounds.
A hole or space covered by a movable piece of board, as in a floor, boxing, partitions, etc.
A cavity in a rock containing a nugget of gold, or other mineral; a small body of ore contained in such a cavity.
A strip of canvas sewn upon a sail so that a batten or a light spar can placed in the interspace.
The pouch of an animal.
The ideal point where the pins are hit by the bowling ball.
A socket for receiving the base of a post, stake, etc.
A bight on a lee shore.
A small space between a tooth and the adjoining gum, formed by an abnormal separation of the two.
To put (something) into a pocket.
To cause a ball to go into one of the pockets of the table; to complete a shot.
To take and keep (something, especially money that is not one's own).

definitions.definition: To shoplift; to steal.

definitions.definition: To put up with; to bear without complaint.
Of a size suitable for putting into a pocket.

definitions.definition: Smaller or more compact than usual.
Referring to the two initial hole cards.

definitions.definition: To open; to unclench.
Nevertheless; yet, still; in spite of (that).

definitions.definition: (degree) To whatever degree or extent
definitions.example: However clear you think you've been, many questions will remain.

definitions.definition: (manner) In whatever way or manner.

definitions.definition: An emphatic form of how.
definitions.example: However were you able to do it?

definitions.definition: In any case, at any rate, at all events.
In whatever way or manner.

definitions.definition: Although, though, but, yet.
To cast out; to banish.
That has been cast out; banished, ostracized.
To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.
definitions.example: The first generation knitted to order; the second still knits for its own use; the next leaves knitting to industrial manufacturers.

definitions.definition: To join closely and firmly together.
definitions.example: The fight for survival knitted the men closely together.

definitions.definition: To become closely and firmly joined; become compacted.
To grow together.
definitions.example: All those seedlings knitted into a kaleidoscopic border.

definitions.definition: To combine from various elements.
definitions.example: The witness knitted together his testimony from contradictory pieces of hearsay.

definitions.definition: Of bones: to heal following a fracture.
definitions.example: I’ll go skiing again after my bones knit.

definitions.definition: To form into a knot, or into knots; to tie together, as cord; to fasten by tying.
To draw together; to contract into wrinkles.
The action of the verb to knit; the process of producing knitted material.
definitions.example: I find knitting very relaxing.

definitions.definition: Material that has been, or is being knitted.
definitions.example: She put down her knitting and went to answer the phone.

definitions.definition: Overhead electrification wires, OHLE
To move with rhythmic steps or movements, especially in time to music.
definitions.example: I danced with her all night long.

definitions.definition: To leap or move lightly and rapidly.
definitions.example: His eyes danced with pleasure as he spoke.   She accused her political opponent of dancing around the issue instead of confronting it.

definitions.definition: To perform the steps to.
definitions.example: Have you ever danced the tango?

definitions.definition: To cause to dance, or move nimbly or merrily about.
To make love or have sex.
definitions.example: You make me feel like dancing.

definitions.definition: The activity of taking part in a dance.
A dance club in France.
Anything that assures a certain outcome.

definitions.definition: A legal assurance of something, e.g. a security for the fulfillment of an obligation.
More specifically, a written declaration that a certain product will be fit for a purpose and work correctly; a warranty
definitions.example: The cooker comes with a five-year guarantee.

definitions.definition: The person to whom a guarantee is made.
A person who gives such a guarantee; a guarantor.
To give an assurance that something will be done right.
To assume or take responsibility for a debt or other obligation.
To make something certain.
definitions.example: The long sunny days guarantee a good crop.

definitions.definition: A musical four-string instrument, generally played with a bow or by plucking the string, with the pitch set by pressing the strings at the appropriate place with the fingers; also any instrument of the violin family.
A violinist.
definitions.example: The first violin often plays the lead melody lines in a string quartet.

definitions.definition: To play on, or as if on, a violin.
A person who lives in, or comes from, a village.
(strategy games) A worker unit.
Recently made, or created.
definitions.example: This is a new scratch on my car!   The band just released a new album.

definitions.definition: Additional; recently discovered.
definitions.example: We turned up some new evidence from the old files.

definitions.definition: Current or later, as opposed to former.
definitions.example: My new car is much better than my previous one, even though it is older.   We had been in our new house for five years by then.

definitions.definition: Used to distinguish something established more recently, named after something or some place previously existing.
definitions.example: New Bond Street is an extension of Bond Street.

definitions.definition: In original condition; pristine; not previously worn or used.
definitions.example: Are you going to buy a new car or a second-hand one?

definitions.definition: Refreshed, reinvigorated, reformed.
definitions.example: That shirt is dirty. Go and put on a new one.   I feel like a new person after a good night's sleep.   After the accident, I saw the world with new eyes.

definitions.definition: Newborn.
definitions.example: My sister has a new baby, and our mother is excited to finally have a grandchild.

definitions.definition: Of recent origin; having taken place recently.
definitions.example: I can't see you for a while; the pain is still too new.   Did you see the new King Lear at the theatre?

definitions.definition: Strange, unfamiliar or not previously known.
definitions.example: The idea was new to me.   I need to meet new people.

definitions.definition: Recently arrived or appeared.
definitions.example: Have you met the new guy in town?   He is the new kid at school.

definitions.definition: Inexperienced or unaccustomed at some task.
definitions.example: Don't worry that you're new at this job; you'll get better with time.   I'm new at this business.

definitions.definition: (of a period of time) Next; about to begin or recently begun.
definitions.example: We expect to grow at 10% annually in the new decade.

definitions.definition: Any of several large carnivorous and carrion-eating birds in the family Accipitridae, having a powerful hooked bill and keen vision.
A gold coin with a face value of ten dollars, formerly used in the United States.
A 13th-century coin minted in Europe and circulated in England as a debased sterling silver penny, outlawed under Edward I.
A score of two under par for a hole.
To score an eagle.
The state or quality of being affable, friendly, or approachable.
Occurring between galaxies.
Arousing suspicion.

definitions.definition: Distrustful or tending to suspect.

definitions.definition: Expressing suspicion
definitions.example: She gave me a suspicious look.

definitions.definition: To take illegally, or without the owner's permission, something owned by someone else.
definitions.example: Three irreplaceable paintings were stolen from the gallery.

definitions.definition: To get or effect surreptitiously or artfully.

definitions.definition: To acquire at a low price.
definitions.example: He stole the car for two thousand less than its book value.

definitions.definition: To draw attention unexpectedly in (an entertainment), especially by being the outstanding performer. Usually used in the phrase steal the show.
To move silently or secretly.

To withdraw or convey (oneself) clandestinely.

To dispossess
To borrow for a short moment.

An Irishman
A Catholic, particularly of Irish descent.

A partially enclosed glass container for displaying plants, especially plants that need high humidity.
An eating establishment in which diners are served food, usually by waiters at their tables but sometimes (as in a fast food restaurant) at a counter.
An alcoholic drink fermented from starch material, commonly barley malt, often with hops or some other substance to impart a bitter flavor.

To gamble.
To play card games, board games, or video games.

The playing of a game or games, including but not limited to video games and games of chance.
Careful, strategic use of rules to achieve one's purposes, as one would use the rules of a game.

The source of a river, information, goods, etc.
